name: Job search
goal: Land aligned conversations and offers by running a disciplined search: clear target, sharp materials, warm outreach, and a repeatable interview loop.
description: Roughly six weeks from “I need to move” to a steady pipeline of applications and interviews. Adjust dates and drop steps that do not apply (e.g. portfolio if you are not in a creative field).

tasks.title: Define target roles, industries, and compensation band
tasks.content:
Clarity beats volume. Write down what you are actually optimizing for.

Decide:
- Titles or role families you will say yes to vs never apply to
- Company size, stage, and remote / hybrid / on-site constraints
- Geographic or timezone limits
- Compensation floor (base and total) and what would make you walk away
- Non-negotiables: benefits, travel, ethics, tech stack, manager style

Output:
- One-page “search brief” you can send to friends and recruiters
- A list of 15–25 target employers (not only job postings)

Done when:
- You can explain your target in two sentences without hedging
- Your brief is in a doc you will update weekly

tasks.title: Align resume, profiles, and proof (portfolio or case sheet)
tasks.content:
Materials should match the brief, not your whole career.

Resume:
- Lead with impact: metrics, scope, outcomes; trim older bullets
- One master resume plus 2–3 variants tuned to role families (save as separate files)

Profiles:
- LinkedIn (or equivalent) headline and about aligned to target roles
- Open-to-work or visibility settings as appropriate for your situation

Proof:
- Portfolio, GitHub readme, or a one-page “greatest hits” case sheet for interviews

Done when:
- A trusted peer has read one variant and understood your positioning in under 60 seconds
- You have a single folder or drive link for “everything a recruiter needs”

tasks.title: Warm network: map, outreach, and informational conversations
tasks.content:
Most strong leads start with people who already trust you.

Map:
- List former managers, peers, clients, alumni, community contacts
- Note who works at or near target employers

Outreach:
- Short, specific asks: advice, intro, or a 20-minute call—not “let me know if you hear of anything”
- Track every touch in a spreadsheet or ATS column

Conversations:
- Prepare 3 questions per chat; send a thank-you with one concrete follow-up

Done when:
- You have held at least five meaningful conversations or have five scheduled
- Your tracker shows who owes you a reply and when to nudge

tasks.title: Application pipeline: volume, tracking, and tailoring
tasks.content:
Treat applications like a funnel with quality gates.

Process:
- Weekly target for new applications (pick a number you can sustain)
- For each role: tailor summary or cover note to one real company problem
- Log job URL, date applied, contact, and status

Quality:
- Skip roles that fail your brief unless you have a deliberate reason
- After rejections, tag pattern: resume, experience gap, timing, unknown

Done when:
- Every active application has a next action and date
- You can report your funnel numbers (applied → screen → onsite → offer) without guessing

tasks.title: Interview prep: stories, questions, and practice
tasks.content:
Reuse prep across employers; refresh specifics the night before.

Stories:
- 5–8 STAR (or equivalent) stories covering conflict, leadership, failure, impact, collaboration
- Technical or domain deep-dives if relevant

Questions:
- Your questions for them: team, success metrics, roadmap, manager style

Practice:
- At least one mock interview with feedback; record yourself for timing and filler

Done when:
- You can deliver your “why this role / why us” answer in under 90 seconds
- You have a cheat sheet per common round (recruiter, hiring manager, panel, executive)

tasks.title: Offers, references, and close-out
tasks.content:
End the search with clean decisions and relationships intact.

References:
- Confirm who is willing; brief them on role and 2–3 themes to reinforce

Offers:
- Compare total comp, growth, commute or travel, and manager fit—not only salary
- Negotiate once with a clear ask and fallback; get material terms in writing

Close-out:
- Thank people who helped; withdraw gracefully from other processes
- Short retro: what worked, what to do earlier next time

Done when:
- Signed offer or documented decision to pause search with criteria to resume
- References and network contacts are thanked
